records.ety: [D. leuteren to delay, loiter; cf; Prov. G. lottern to be louse, lotter louse, slack, unsettled, vagrant, OHG. lotar.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Loitered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Loitering.]

defs: To be slow in moving; to delay; to linger; to be dilatory; to spend time idly; to saunter; to lag behind.
quotes: Sir John, you loiter here too long.
If we have loitered, let us quicken our pace.

defs: To wander as an idle vagrant.
